A path can go through any node. It can go left-child -> node -> right-child (a V-shape), or it can be just a downward branch.
For each node, compute the maximum 'gain' you can get by including it in a downward path. If the gain from a child is negative, it's better to not include that subtree.
For each node, compute the potential path sum that passes THROUGH that node by combining left gain + node.val + right gain. Update the global maximum.
The key distinction: the function returns only a ONE-SIDED max gain (going down either left OR right), because that's the only thing that can be extended upward to a parent node.
